mnemonics.title: PPP Three Phases Memory
mnemonics.content: Link Authentication Network - Remember as 'LAN' where Link comes first, then Authentication (optional), then Network layer works.

mnemonics.title: LCP Functions
mnemonics.content: L-C-P: Link testing, Configuration, Protocol termination - Remember as 'Learn Configure Properly'

acronyms.usage: Secure three-way handshake authentication using MD5
Maximum frame size receiver can accept

visualTips.visualization: Draw a train with 7 carriages: Flag (engine), Address (coal car), Control (passenger), Protocol (cargo), Information (main cargo), FCS (brake), Flag (caboose). All PPP frames start and end with the 7E flag.
Think of 0x7E as a 'flag' that marks boundaries. If the flag appears INSIDE the train (information field), it must be 'disguised' by preceding it with an escape character (0x7D) and XORing with 0x20.
Think of building a house: Phase 1 = laying foundation (link establishment), Phase 2 = security system installation (authentication), Phase 3 = moving in and setting up utilities (network layer).

analogies.analogy: HDLC is like a single-purpose road that only allows one type of vehicle (protocol), while PPP is like a multi-lane highway that allows cars, trucks, buses (multiple protocols) by using lane markers (Protocol field).
LCP is like the construction crew that builds and tests the road connection, while NCP is like the traffic management system that decides how different vehicles (IP, IPX) will use the road.
CHAP is like a bank security question - the server 'challenges' you with a random question, you provide an answer only you know (hashed), preventing eavesdroppers from stealing your actual password.
Protocol field is like an envelope's address label - it tells the post office (data link layer) which department (network layer protocol) should receive the letter (data).
